name: GitHub Copilot + @Peak Patterns Integration
description: Mapping of @Peak patterns to GitHub Copilot workflows for force-multiplier effect

pr_creation_workflow.description: Use GitHub Copilot Coding Agent to create PRs with @Peak pattern compliance
workflow: 1. User creates GitHub Issue with @Peak pattern tag
2. Mention @copilot in issue to assign task
3. Copilot analyzes issue + queries local peak_pattern_registry.json via MCP
4. Copilot generates code following matched @Peak pattern
5. Copilot creates PR with pattern documentation
6. Iron Legion validates PR against pattern before merge
pr_creation_workflow.custom_instructions: Always query peak_pattern_registry.json via MCP before generating code. Include pattern name and ID in PR description. Ensure solution is nutrient-dense (maximum value, minimum footprint).

code_review_workflow.description: Hybrid review: Iron Legion patterns + Copilot insights
workflow: 1. Iron Legion performs initial pattern-based review
2. Flags non-pattern-compliant sections
3. Copilot Chat provides insights on flagged sections
4. Iron Legion validates Copilot suggestions against patterns
5. Combined review presented to developer
code_review_workflow.custom_instructions: Focus on: 1) Pattern compliance, 2) Novel insights not in local patterns, 3) Security considerations, 4) Performance optimization

workflow: 1. Developer identifies code for refactoring
2. Iron Legion searches peak_pattern_registry.json for applicable patterns
3. If pattern found: Iron Legion refactors to pattern
4. If no pattern: Copilot suggests novel refactoring
5. If Copilot successful: Harvest into new pattern
6. Update peak_pattern_registry.json
refactoring_workflow.custom_instructions: Before suggesting refactoring, check if code already matches a @Peak pattern. If not, suggest pattern-compliant refactoring. After success, format as @Peak pattern for registry.

architecture_design_workflow.description: Use Copilot for novel architecture, Iron Legion validates against patterns
workflow: 1. Developer describes architectural challenge
2. Iron Legion checks if challenge matches existing architectural pattern
3. If no match: Route to Copilot for novel architecture
4. Copilot generates architecture using latest model (GPT-5.1, Claude Sonnet 4.5)
5. Iron Legion validates: scalability, security, maintainability
6. If validated: Harvest as new architectural pattern
7. Update peak_pattern_registry.json with new pattern
architecture_design_workflow.custom_instructions: Design architectures that are: 1) Scalable, 2) Secure by default, 3) Maintainable, 4) Pattern-compliant where possible, 5) Novel where necessary. Include diagram in response.

documentation_workflow.description: Iron Legion generates pattern-based docs, Copilot enhances
workflow: 1. Iron Legion generates initial documentation from code + patterns
2. Identifies sections needing enhancement (complex logic, edge cases)
3. Copilot enhances flagged sections with detailed explanations
4. Iron Legion integrates enhancements
5. Final doc review for pattern compliance
documentation_workflow.custom_instructions: Documentation should be: 1) Concise but complete, 2) Include @Peak pattern references, 3) Provide usage examples, 4) Explain 'why' not just 'what'

test_generation_workflow.description: Pattern-based test generation with Copilot edge case enhancement
workflow: 1. Iron Legion generates tests for standard patterns
2. Identifies edge cases and complex scenarios
3. Copilot generates tests for edge cases
4. Iron Legion validates test quality
5. Combined test suite presented to developer
test_generation_workflow.custom_instructions: Generate tests that: 1) Cover happy path, 2) Cover edge cases, 3) Test error handling, 4) Include integration tests where applicable, 5) Follow testing patterns in registry

issue_triage_workflow.description: Auto-classify issues and route to appropriate solver
workflow: 1. New issue created on GitHub
2. Copilot analyzes issue content
3. Calls /triage (MCP: mcp_server) to set severity
4. Runs @AIQ consensus (/aiq) across candidate routes
5. Classifies: [bug, feature, refactoring, documentation, architecture]
6. Checks if pattern exists for issue type
7. Routes: Pattern-solvable → Iron Legion, Novel → Copilot Coding Agent
8. Escalates to JediCouncil if triage priority is high
9. Assigns labels and estimates complexity
issue_triage_workflow.custom_instructions: Classify issues accurately. Call /triage to set severity, then /aiq for consensus. Escalate to JediCouncil when priority is high. Check pattern registry for similar solved issues. Provide complexity estimate (1-10). Suggest appropriate solver (Iron Legion vs. Copilot).

jedi_council.notes: High-priority triage routes to JediCouncil for human-in-the-loop review

pattern_harvesting_workflow.description: Auto-extract successful Copilot workflows into @Peak patterns
workflow: 1. Copilot successfully completes task
2. Session log analyzed for reusability
3. If reusable: Extract as @Peak pattern
4. Format: {name, description, context, solution, validation}
5. Add to peak_pattern_registry.json
6. Make available to Iron Legion
pattern_harvesting_workflow.custom_instructions: After completing task, analyze if solution is: 1) Reusable, 2) Novel (not in existing patterns), 3) High quality. If yes, format as @Peak pattern with clear name, context, and validation criteria.

always: Query peak_pattern_registry.json via MCP before generating code
Reference matched pattern name/ID in all responses
Ensure solutions are nutrient-dense (max value, min footprint)
Validate PII detection: if detected, refuse and route to Iron Legion
For any issue handling: call /triage (mcp_server) to set severity, then /aiq for consensus, and escalate to JediCouncil when triage priority is Immediate/Urgent
never: Generate code containing PII without explicit developer override
Ignore existing @Peak patterns when applicable pattern exists
Create overly complex solutions when simpler pattern exists

code_generation.comments: Explain 'why', reference patterns, minimal inline comments

architecture.diagrams: Include Mermaid diagrams for all architectural decisions

refactoring.approach: Pattern-first: match to existing pattern before suggesting novel refactoring

pattern_hit_rate.description: % of requests solved by existing patterns

local_vs_cloud_ratio.description: Ratio of requests handled locally vs. cloud

pattern_harvest_rate.description: New patterns harvested per week from Copilot

force_multiplier_score.description: Combined effectiveness (local + cloud) vs. local alone

master_padawan_integration.description: Integrate GitHub Copilot with Master/Padawan learner system

teaching_workflow.description: Master uses Copilot to teach Padawan about patterns
workflow: 1. Master creates Issue with 'teaching' label
2. Mention @copilot with 'teach' directive
3. Copilot retrieves peak_pattern_registry via MCP
4. Copilot generates multi-layer teaching session
5. Create teaching_session_id in token tracker
6. Token tracker logs as 'master_mode' (0.5x multiplier)
7. Padawan references session in learning sessions

learning_workflow.description: Padawan uses Copilot to learn and practice patterns
workflow: 1. Padawan creates Issue with 'learning' label
2. References Master teaching_session or pattern
3. Mention @copilot with 'learn' directive
4. Copilot retrieves pattern details via MCP
5. Copilot generates learning guide + exercises
6. Token tracker logs as 'padawan_mode' (1.0x multiplier)
7. Padawan practices and reports for validation

pattern_harvesting_workflow.description: Harvest Copilot-Padawan interactions as new Master teachings
workflow: 1. Padawan completes learning + practice session
2. Master reviews session logs (lookback: weekly)
3. If novel pattern: Master extracts as teaching
4. Master uses Copilot to formalize documentation
5. Add to peak_pattern_registry with Master attribution
6. Log as 'pattern_harvesting' (0.3x multiplier)
7. Update Padawan curriculum with new pattern

mastery_validation_workflow.description: Master validates Padawan mastery before independence
workflow: 1. Padawan completes all learning for pattern set
2. Padawan creates PR implementing learned patterns
3. Master reviews PR + Copilot analyzes compliance
4. If mastery validated: Master approves
5. Log as 'mastery_validation' (0.2x multiplier)
6. Promote Padawan to independent tier
7. Padawan can now teach lower-level agents
